techniques.name: Be clear and direct
techniques.guidance: Apply the colleague test: if a colleague with minimal context would be confused, so will the model. State the desired output explicitly; replace vague quantifiers with measures.

techniques.name: Add context and motivation
techniques.guidance: State who the output is for, what it enables, and why constraints exist. Give the reason, not only the request — models connect tasks to relevant knowledge through stated intent.

techniques.name: Use examples (multishot)
techniques.guidance: Provide 3-5 relevant, diverse examples wrapped in example tags when output style or structure matters. Examples steer format more reliably than descriptions.

techniques.name: Structure with XML tags
techniques.guidance: Separate instructions, context, input data, and examples with consistent, descriptive tags so no text is ambiguous between 'instruction' and 'material to operate on'.

techniques.name: Give the model a role
techniques.guidance: A one-sentence role statement focuses behavior and tone for the domain.

techniques.name: Order long context correctly
techniques.guidance: Place long documents and data at the top, the query and instructions at the end, and ask for quote-grounding when working over long material.

techniques.name: Specify output positively
techniques.guidance: Say what the output should be (format, structure, sections) rather than what it should not be; match the prompt's own style to the desired output style.

techniques.name: Decompose or chain complex tasks
techniques.guidance: Split unrelated goals into separate prompts; for dependent multi-step work, state the sequence or chain prompts so intermediate output can be inspected.

techniques.name: Define success criteria and constraints
techniques.guidance: State what 'done' looks like and every constraint you actually hold (length, tone, technology, boundaries) so success is checkable from the prompt alone.

techniques.name: Avoid over-prescription
techniques.guidance: Current models follow instructions literally. Replace emphatic scaffolding (ALL CAPS, CRITICAL/MUST chains, exhaustive step lists) with goals and constraints; over-prescription degrades output.
